summary: Provision AWS Amplify Hosting for a new frontend: the app container that owns a repository and build settings, and a branch that deploys from it. Both are engineer-gated creates — repository access credentials are never captured here, and Amplify's own IAM/build behavior needs a review before it goes live.

operations.title: Create a new Amplify app
operations.summary: Request a new Amplify Hosting app for a frontend repository. An engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it's applied.
operations.description: Specify a new aws_amplify_app: its name, the repository it deploys from, and the hosting platform. The repository access credential (access_token or oauth_token) is never captured here — the engineer connects it out of band, the same way any other CI integration credential is handled. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: app_name
params.label: App name
params.help: The app's name in the Amplify console.

params.name: repository
params.label: Repository URL
params.help: The Git repository URL (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, or CodeCommit). Leave blank to connect a repository later from the console.

params.name: platform
params.label: Platform
params.help: WEB is a static site; WEB_COMPUTE adds server-side rendering (e.g. Next.js); WEB_DYNAMIC is the newer dynamic-rendering platform.

params.name: enable_branch_auto_build
params.label: Auto-build new commits
params.help: Automatically starts a build whenever a connected branch receives a new commit.

params.name: enable_branch_auto_deletion
params.label: Auto-delete backend on branch delete
params.help: Deletes the deployed backend environment automatically when its branch is deleted from the repository.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: App name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this app is
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this app.

decisions: The repository access token (access_token for GitHub, or oauth_token for other providers) is never captured here — the engineer connects the repository out of band, exactly like any other CI integration credential
A custom build_spec (amplify.yml), environment variables, and a service role for server-side rendering are engineer follow-ups after creation
Custom domains (the companion aws_amplify_domain_association) and auto-branch-creation patterns are engineer decisions

operations.title: Create a new Amplify branch
operations.summary: Request a new deploy branch for an existing Amplify app. An engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it's applied.
operations.description: Specify a new aws_amplify_branch bound to an existing app: which Git branch it tracks, its deploy stage, and whether commits auto-build. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: app
params.label: Amplify app
params.help: The Amplify app this branch deploys into. Create the app first (amplify-app-create) if the list is empty.

params.name: branch_name
params.label: Branch name
params.help: Must match the Git branch name exactly.

params.name: stage
params.label: Stage
params.help: Amplify's deploy-stage label for this branch. Shown in the console and affects default performance/PR-preview behavior.

params.name: enable_auto_build
params.label: Auto-build new commits
params.help: Automatically starts a build whenever this branch receives a new commit.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Branch name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this branch deploys
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this branch.

decisions: Environment variables that differ from the app-level defaults are an engineer follow-up
A custom domain / subdomain mapping (the companion aws_amplify_domain_association) is an engineer decision
Framework auto-detection is left to Amplify; overriding it is an engineer follow-up if detection misfires
